answer.text: A company carving out one or more existing businesses into a separate company
A company buying back its own shares
Two companies merging into one entity

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In a spin-off, a company carves out a business into a separate entity. Shareholders receive shares in the new company proportional to their holding. **The Trap:** Acquiring is M&A. Buyback reduces shares. Merging combines entities. Spin-off separates them. **Cross-Exam Context:** Demerger mechanics tested in NISM XV Ch 9. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Adani Enterprise spun off Adani Green Energy, Adani Ports, and Adani Transmission — each becoming independently listed entities.
